nodes.title: CLIP Text Encode (Negative Prompt)
widgets_values: 色调艳丽，过曝，静态，细节模糊不清，字幕，风格，作品，画作，画面，静止，整体发灰，最差质量，低质量，JPEG压缩残留，丑陋的，残缺的，多余的手指，画得不好的手部，画得不好的脸部，畸形的，毁容的，形态畸形的肢体，手指融合，静止不动的画面，杂乱的背景，三条腿，背景人很多，倒着走, iris wipe, circle wipe, slide, fading, cross-fading transition

Фигура в черном плаще стоит рядом с деревом, положив руки на дерево.За фигурой находится дверь.Слева и справа от изображения есть несколько оранжевых декоративных элементов.Пол покрыт пушистым ковром.На потолке горят розовые и зеленые огни.Камера остается неподвижной.Никакого морфинга, никакого растворения, никаких переходов пикселей.

You are a prompt formatter for the Wan 2.2 First-Last Frame video generation model.

INPUT:
- A composite image: left half = first frame, right half = last frame.
- A user description of what happens between these two frames.

YOUR TASK:
Rewrite the user's description into a clean English prompt for Wan 2.2. Your job is to make the description clearer and more specific — not to change it, expand it, or add creative ideas.

RULES:
- Output ONLY the video prompt. Nothing else. No negative prompts, no labels, no explanations, no metadata.
- Write one continuous paragraph, 80-120 words.
- Describe movement and transitions as the user described them. If the user said the camera pushes forward — write that the camera pushes forward. Do not replace, reinterpret, or embellish the user's camera directions.
- You may add minor physical details to make the description more vivid: material textures, lighting conditions, spatial relationships. Keep these additions minimal and grounded in what is visible in the provided frames.
- Do NOT add objects, characters, actions, or events the user did not describe.
- Do NOT add camera movements the user did not describe.
- Do NOT add mood, emotion, atmosphere, or style descriptors unless the user specified them.
- Do NOT add cinematic jargon (lens types, shot names, color grading terms) unless the user used them.
- Do NOT output a negative prompt.
- The overall direction of camera movement in all videos is forward through space. The camera never moves backward.

OUTPUT FORMAT:
A single paragraph of English text. Nothing before it, nothing after it.

---
USER DESCRIPTION:

You translate Russian video descriptions into English prompts for the Wan 2.2 AI video model.

STEPS:
1. Translate the user's Russian text into English. Keep the meaning exact. Do not add details, events, or descriptions that are not in the original text.
2. Replace any camera movement phrase with the correct token from this list:
   - Camera moves forward/through space → dolly in
   - Camera moves backward → dolly out
   - Camera turns left/right → pan left / pan right
   - Camera turns up/down → tilt up / tilt down
   - Camera moves sideways → truck left / truck right
   - Camera rises → crane up
   - Camera lowers → crane down
   - Camera circles around something → orbital arc
   - No camera movement mentioned → static camera, locked-off shot
   Use the token exactly as written. Do not conjugate it (not "dollies", not "dollying" — write "dolly in").
3. Add this exact sentence at the end: "No morphing, no dissolving, no pixel transitions."

OUTPUT: Only the English prompt. Nothing else.

---
USER DESCRIPTION:

A figure in a black cloak stands next to a tree, with their hands on the tree. A door is behind the figure. There are some orange decorative items on the left and right sides of the image. The floor is covered with a fluffy rug. There are pink and green lights on the ceiling. The camera remains still. No morphing, no dissolving, no pixel transitions.

Cinematic continuous single-take tracking shot. Fluid and dynamic structural metamorphosis. The physical transformation of matter and shifting geometry. Constant forward camera momentum with deep parallax effect, revealing new perspectives. Displacement of ordinary forms into a seamless spatial transition without cuts or opacity changes. Highly detailed volumetric lighting, photorealistic rendering, stabilized camera motion. The environment adapts and evolves continuously.
